steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to contrast the orbits of Iapetus and Mimas using information from the notes. A contrast requires showing a meaningful difference between the two moons, grounded in data the notes actually provide.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Iapetus's orbit of Saturn is elliptical in shape and takes, on average, 79.33 Earth days, while Mimas's orbit of Saturn is circular in shape and takes, on average, 0.94 Earth days.’: the notes say Kepler described orbits as elliptical — they never say Mimas’s orbit is circular. This invents an unsupported distinction. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Kepler's three laws of planetary motion describe the orbits of planets and natural satellites, including those of Iapetus and Mimas.’: describes something the two moons share (both follow Kepler’s laws), not a contrast between them. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Mimas completes its orbit of Saturn many times faster than Iapetus does.’: directly contrasts the two moons using the orbital-period data from the notes (0.94 days vs. 79.33 days). ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Saturn's moons, specifically Iapetus and Mimas, orbit their planet in the same way the planets orbit the Sun.’: describes a similarity (both follow the same orbital mechanics), not a contrast between the two moons. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
